revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)]]</includeonly>

'''鹿児島神社'''（かごしまじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[垂水市]]下宮町にある神社。別名「'''下宮神社'''」
== 沿革 == [[枚聞神社]]から勧請されたという説と[[鹿児島神宮]]の神領に勧請されたという説がある。 成立時期は不明だが、[[鹿児島神宮]]、[[鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|鹿児島市の鹿児島神社]]と深く関連のある神社であり、[[桜島]]をご神体とする神社と考えられている。またおなじ垂水市内で「上宮神社」を称する[[手貫神社]]とのつながりが深い。
== 祭神 == *[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]] *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫尊]] *[[タマヨリヒメ|玉依姫尊]] *[[サルタヒコ|塩司翁・猿田彦命]] *[[天智天皇]] *大宮姫 ※地元伝承では天智天皇の后の一人とされる。
== 関連項目 == *[[鹿児島神宮]] *[[鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]
[[Category:鹿児島県の神社|かこしましんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

'''鹿児島神社'''（かごしまじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[垂水市]]下宮町にある神社。別名「'''下宮神社'''」
== 沿革 == [[枚聞神社]]から勧請されたという説と[[鹿児島神宮]]の神領に勧請されたという説がある。 成立時期は不明だが、[[鹿児島神宮]]、[[鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|鹿児島市の鹿児島神社]]と深く関連のある神社であり、[[桜島]]をご神体とする神社と考えられている。またおなじ垂水市内で「上宮神社」を称する[[手貫神社]]とのつながりが深い。
境内には「軍艦マーチ」の作曲者である[[瀬戸口藤吉]]の記念碑がある。
== 祭神 == *[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]] *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫尊]] *[[タマヨリヒメ|玉依姫尊]] *[[サルタヒコ|塩司翁・猿田彦命]] *[[天智天皇]] *大宮姫 ※地元伝承では天智天皇の后の一人とされる。
== 関連項目 == *[[鹿児島神宮]] *[[鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]
[[Category:鹿児島県の神社|かこしましんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%9E%82%E6%B0%B4%E5%B8%82)]]

[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%9E%82%E6%B0%B4%E5%B8%82)]] {{Citations needed|date=June 12, 2015}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |Image = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|Exterior (March 2022)]] |Location = 88 Minamimatsubara-chō, Tarumizu City, Kagoshima Prefecture |ISO = JP-46 |LatitudeDegrees = 31 |LatitudeMinutes = 29 |LatitudeSeconds = 13.8 |LongitudeDegrees = 130 |LongitudeMinutes = 41 |LongitudeSeconds =57.1 |Kami = Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Late Nara period (traditional) |Honden = |Alias = Shimomiya Shrine |Fudasho = |Annual Festival = November 3 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu City|ja|垂水市}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} *{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島}} as its {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Setoguchi Tōkichi|ja|瀬戸口藤吉}}, composer of the "{{ill|Gunkan kōshinkyoku|ja|軍艦行進曲}}" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
{{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu City|ja|垂水市}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} *{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島}} as its {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Setoguchi Tōkichi|ja|瀬戸口藤吉}}, composer of the "{{ill|Gunkan kōshinkyoku|ja|軍艦行進曲}}" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumizu City]] [[Category:History of Tarumizu City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:鹿児島神社 (垂水市)]]

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu City|ja|垂水市}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} *{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島}} as its {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Setoguchi Tōkichi|ja|瀬戸口藤吉}}, composer of the "{{ill|Gunkan kōshinkyoku|ja|軍艦行進曲}}" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumizu City]] [[Category:History of Tarumizu City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:鹿児島神社 (垂水市)]]

== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=draft}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊|12=draft}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊|12=draft}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年6月12日 (金) 19:03 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 鹿児島神社 |画像 = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|外観(2022年3月)]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県垂水市南松原町88 |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 31 |緯度分 = 29 |緯度秒 = 13.8 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 41 |経度秒 =57.1 |祭神 = 彦火火出見尊ほか |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・奈良時代末期 |本殿 = |別名 = 下宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''鹿児島神社'''（かごしまじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[垂水市]]南松原町にある[[神社]]。別名「'''下宮神社'''」。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
== 祭神 == *[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]] *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫尊]] *[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫尊]] *[[サルタヒコ|塩司翁・猿田彦命]] *[[天智天皇]] *大宮姫 ※地元伝承では天智天皇の后の一人とされる。
== 由緒 == [[奈良時代]]末期に創建されたと伝えられる。[[枚聞神社]]から[[勧請]]されたという説と[[鹿児島神宮]]の[[神領]]に勧請されたという説がある。 成立時期は不明だが、[[鹿児島神宮]]、[[鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|鹿児島市の鹿児島神社]]と深く関連のある神社であり、[[桜島]]を[[神体]]とする神社と考えられている。またおなじ垂水市内で「上宮神社」を称する[[手貫神社]]とのつながりが深い。
== 境内 == 「[[軍艦行進曲]]」の作曲者である[[瀬戸口藤吉]]の記念碑がある。
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしましんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:垂水市の建築物]] [[Category:垂水市の歴史]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿児島神社 (垂水市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tarumizu|qid=Q847591}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.487166666666667|N|130.69919444444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=June 12, 2015}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|Exterior (March 2022)]] |Location = 88 Minamimatsubara-chō, Tarumizu City, Kagoshima Prefecture |ISO = JP-46 |LatitudeDegrees = 31 |LatitudeMinutes = 29 |LatitudeSeconds = 13.8 |LongitudeDegrees = 130 |LongitudeMinutes = 41 |LongitudeSeconds =57.1 |Kami = Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Late Nara period (traditional) |Honden = |Alias = Shimomiya Shrine |Fudasho = |Annual Festival = November 3 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, [[:en:Tarumizu, Kagoshima|Tarumizu City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]], while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]].
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿児島神社 (垂水市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tarumizu|WD=Q847591}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.487166666666667|N|130.69919444444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11676981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=June 12, 2015}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|Exterior (March 2022)]] |Location = 88 Minamimatsubara-chō, Tarumizu City, Kagoshima Prefecture |ISO = JP-46 |LatitudeDegrees = 31 |LatitudeMinutes = 29 |LatitudeSeconds = 13.8 |LongitudeDegrees = 130 |LongitudeMinutes = 41 |LongitudeSeconds =57.1 |Kami = Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Late Nara period (traditional) |Honden = |Alias = Shimomiya Shrine |Fudasho = |Annual Festival = November 3 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, [[:en:Tarumizu, Kagoshima|Tarumizu City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]], while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]].
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, [[:en:Tarumizu, Kagoshima|Tarumizu City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]], while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]].
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿児島神社 (垂水市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tarumizu|WD=Q847591}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.487166666666667|N|130.69919444444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11676981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=June 12, 2015}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|Exterior (March 2022)]] |Location = 88 Minamimatsubara-chō, Tarumizu City, Kagoshima Prefecture |ISO = JP-46 |LatitudeDegrees = 31 |LatitudeMinutes = 29 |LatitudeSeconds = 13.8 |LongitudeDegrees = 130 |LongitudeMinutes = 41 |LongitudeSeconds =57.1 |Kami = Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Late Nara period (traditional) |Honden = |Alias = Shimomiya Shrine |Fudasho = |Annual Festival = November 3 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, [[:en:Tarumizu, Kagoshima|Tarumizu City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]], while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]].
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, [[:en:Tarumizu, Kagoshima|Tarumizu City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]], while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]].
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, [[:en:Tarumizu, Kagoshima|Tarumizu City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] *[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]], while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]].
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] as its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to [[:en:Tokichi Setoguchi|Setoguchi Tōkichi]], composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a Village Shrine.

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|en|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|WD=Q847591|lt=Tarumizu City}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirasaki Shrine|en|Hirasaki Shrine|WD=Q3136198|lt=Hirakiki Shrine}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|en|Sakurajima|WD=Q128841|lt=Sakurajima}} as its {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Tokichi Setoguchi|en|Tokichi Setoguchi|WD=Q856996|lt=Setoguchi Tōkichi}}, composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |{{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

{{Citations needed|date=June 12, 2015}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|Exterior (March 2022)]] |Location = 88 Minamimatsubara-chō, Tarumizu City, Kagoshima Prefecture |ISO = JP-46 |LatitudeDegrees = 31 |LatitudeMinutes = 29 |LatitudeSeconds = 13.8 |LongitudeDegrees = 130 |LongitudeMinutes = 41 |LongitudeSeconds =57.1 |Kami = Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Late Nara period (traditional) |Honden = |Alias = Shimomiya Shrine |Fudasho = |Annual Festival = November 3 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|en|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|WD=Q847591|lt=Tarumizu City}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirasaki Shrine|en|Hirasaki Shrine|WD=Q3136198|lt=Hirakiki Shrine}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|en|Sakurajima|WD=Q128841|lt=Sakurajima}} as its {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Tokichi Setoguchi|en|Tokichi Setoguchi|WD=Q856996|lt=Setoguchi Tōkichi}}, composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |{{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

{{Citations needed|date=June 12, 2015}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagoshima shrine Tarumizu Kagoshima.jpg|thumb|Exterior (March 2022)]] |Location = 88 Minamimatsubara-chō, Tarumizu City, Kagoshima Prefecture |ISO = JP-46 |LatitudeDegrees = 31 |LatitudeMinutes = 29 |LatitudeSeconds = 13.8 |LongitudeDegrees = 130 |LongitudeMinutes = 41 |LongitudeSeconds =57.1 |Kami = Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Late Nara period (traditional) |Honden = |Alias = Shimomiya Shrine |Fudasho = |Annual Festival = November 3 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima }}
'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|en|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|WD=Q847591|lt=Tarumizu City|1=Tarumizu, Kagoshima}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirasaki Shrine|en|Hirasaki Shrine|WD=Q3136198|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|1=Hirasaki Shrine}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū|1=Kagoshima Jingū}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū|1=Kagoshima Jingū}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|en|Sakurajima|WD=Q128841|lt=Sakurajima|1=Sakurajima}} as its {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Tokichi Setoguchi|en|Tokichi Setoguchi|WD=Q856996|lt=Setoguchi Tōkichi|1=Tokichi Setoguchi}}, composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
== Comparison with the other Kagoshima Shrines == {| class="wikitable" |+In clockwise order around the Island !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|WD=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū|1=Kagoshima Jingū}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2">[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.</ref> |yes<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=由緒について - 鹿児島神宮|url=https://kagoshima-jingu.jp/yuisyo.php|access-date=2022-09-28|website=kagoshima-jingu.jp}}</ref> |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |yes<ref name=":0" /> |yes |- |{{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes<ref name="Shrine Association2" /> |no |no |- |{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes<ref name=":0" /> |no |- |{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |- | | | | |}

The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|en|Sakurajima|WD=Q128841|lt=Sakurajima|1=Sakurajima}} as its {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Tokichi Setoguchi|en|Tokichi Setoguchi|WD=Q856996|lt=Setoguchi Tōkichi|1=Tokichi Setoguchi}}, composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.

'''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Minamimatsubara-chō, {{ill|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|en|Tarumizu, Kagoshima|qid=Q847591|lt=Tarumizu City|1=Tarumizu, Kagoshima}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Shimomiya Shrine'''. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a Village Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == *{{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|qid=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}} *{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} *Ōmiya-hime (traditionally said to be one of Emperor Tenji's consorts)
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the late {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. There are two theories regarding its establishment: one suggests it was transferred from {{ill|Hirasaki Shrine|en|Hirasaki Shrine|qid=Q3136198|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|1=Hirasaki Shrine}}, while another posits that it was established in the sacred territory of {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|qid=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū|1=Kagoshima Jingū}}.
Although its precise founding date is unknown, it has strong historical ties to {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|qid=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Jingū|1=Kagoshima Jingū}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}}. The shrine is thought to have originally worshipped {{ill|Sakurajima|en|Sakurajima|qid=Q128841|lt=Sakurajima|1=Sakurajima}} as its {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|qid=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}}. Additionally, it maintains a strong connection to {{ill|Tekan Shrine|ja|手貫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tekan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another shrine in Tarumizu City known locally as "Kamimiya Shrine."
== Shrine Grounds == A commemorative monument dedicated to {{ill|Tokichi Setoguchi|en|Tokichi Setoguchi|qid=Q856996|lt=Setoguchi Tōkichi|1=Tokichi Setoguchi}}, composer of the "[[:en:Gunkan kōshinkyoku|Gunkan kōshinkyoku]]" (Warship March), is located within the shrine grounds.
